label: About
title: About Twin Lens Air Earth Observatory
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 15 recent saved-signal files and 16 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around Air / Earth, by Celia Hollander and Takashi Kokubo (小久保隆) - Blessings Of The Forest (森の恵み) (199X) [Full Album], plus 7 more. A source image from f4.bcbits.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Twin Lens Air Earth Observatory: Create a finished still image of a daybreak listening observatory built from two large overlapping circular apertures, like twin lenses set into open air. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against upper translucent sky lens, lower moss and soil lens, and embedded circular mineral plaques.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
